title: Smart Electric Meter Market Report 2026-2035, Growing at 8.27% CAGR to Reach USD 59.22 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global Smart Electric Meter Market is projected to grow from USD 27.24 billion in 2025 to USD 59.22 billion by 2035, expanding at a CAGR of 8.27%. Growth is driven by smart grid modernization, utility digitalization, and advanced metering infrastructure deployment. Asia Pacific leads with 45.19% market share, while single-phase smart meters dominate at 68.29% of revenue. Key players include Landis+Gyr, Itron Inc., Siemens AG, and Schneider Electric.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company launched upgraded cellular and RF mesh-enabled smart meters with enhanced interoperability and cloud-based platforms, demonstrating active innovation and alignment with market growth drivers in utility digitization.

Expanded smart meter portfolio with next-generation AMI solutions featuring AI-powered grid analytics and real-time outage detection, positioning the company to capitalize on growing demand for advanced metering infrastructure.
Listed among leading market players in the expanding smart electric meter market driven by smart grid modernization and utility digitalization investments.
Listed as a leading market player in the growing smart electric meter market, benefiting from global smart grid investments and utility modernization trends.
Identified as a key market player positioned to benefit from the 8.27% CAGR growth in smart metering solutions and smart grid digitalization initiatives globally.

title: Should You Buy the Honeywell Aerospace Spinoff?
author: Thomas Niel
description: Honeywell International is splitting into Honeywell Aerospace and Honeywell Technologies on June 29, 2026. The spinoff aims to unlock shareholder value by allowing each pure-play company to receive higher valuations than the diversified conglomerate currently trades at. Honeywell Aerospace is expected to benefit from the hot aerospace sector, while Honeywell Technologies could present a value opportunity if it experiences post-spinoff weakness.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The spinoff is expected to unlock shareholder value through valuation expansion. Honeywell trades at 21.6x forward earnings versus competitors at 30-46x, suggesting significant upside potential if the two companies achieve partial multiple expansion. Management expects double-digit earnings growth post-spinoff.

Expected to benefit from strong aerospace sector tailwinds with 6-8% annual sales growth guidance. The aerospace sector is described as 'hot,' and the pure-play structure could attract investors seeking aerospace exposure, potentially driving share appreciation.

Mentioned as a comparable pure-play aerospace competitor trading at 46x forward earnings. Used as a valuation benchmark to illustrate potential upside for Honeywell Aerospace, but no direct investment recommendation provided.
Referenced as an automation-focused industrial comparable trading at 30x forward earnings. Used as a valuation benchmark for Honeywell Technologies but carries no direct investment thesis.

title: SBA Celebrates Success of Detroit Supplier Matchmaking Expo
author: U.S. Small Business Administration
description: The SBA held its 2026 Detroit Supplier Matchmaking Expo on May 5, 2026, bringing together over 600 small business suppliers and three dozen large industrial buyers including the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, NASA, Ford, Google, Honeywell, Toyota, and Siemens. The event resulted in 140 successful matches, with 48% of participants expecting over $250,000 in new business opportunities and 82% reporting satisfaction with the networking outcomes.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Co-hosted the Expo event, demonstrating commitment to sourcing from domestic small suppliers and supporting the Made in America agenda.
Participated as a large industrial buyer at the Expo, actively seeking to connect with small domestic suppliers to expand production capacity.

title: 3 Quantum Computing Stocks Down Sharply -- but 1 Offers Exceptional Value
author: Micah Zimmerman
description: Quantum computing stocks have experienced a valuation reset after a strong rally in April-May. While Rigetti Computing and IonQ show impressive technology and growth, their valuations appear stretched. D-Wave Quantum stands out as offering better value with strong booking momentum, major enterprise contracts, and a more reasonable valuation that may underestimate its commercial potential.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While technology is advancing, revenue base is too thin ($4.4M in Q1) to justify current valuation with confidence. Stock is down 19% YTD and market cap implies commercial traction not yet demonstrated. Analyst is watching but not buying.

While technology is advancing, revenue base is too thin ($4.4M in Q1) to justify current valuation with confidence. Stock is down 19% YTD and market cap implies commercial traction not yet demonstrated. Analyst is watching but not buying.

Best fundamentals in pure-play quantum space with exceptional 755% YoY revenue growth, $470M backlog, and raised guidance. However, stock trades at premium valuation that prices in years of perfect execution. Market is charging investors to own the fundamentals rather than paying them.

Best fundamentals in pure-play quantum space with exceptional 755% YoY revenue growth, $470M backlog, and raised guidance. However, stock trades at premium valuation that prices in years of perfect execution. Market is charging investors to own the fundamentals rather than paying them.

Strongest value proposition with 1,994% YoY booking growth, $33.4M in closed bookings, major enterprise contracts ($20M and $10M deals), and only dual-platform quantum company. Trading at ~$23 with 20% YTD decline, market appears to be pricing in yesterday's skepticism while ignoring current booking momentum.
Mentioned as better-funded competitor with $12.7B valuation and Honeywell backing. IPO filing reminded investors of competitive landscape but limited operational details provided in article.

title: Here's Why Honeywell Stock Popped Higher Today
author: Lee Samaha
description: Honeywell International's stock rose 4.4% after its board formally approved the spinoff of Honeywell Aerospace, completing the company's breakup into two focused entities: Honeywell Aerospace (aerospace and defense) and Honeywell Technologies (building, process, and industrial automation). Both companies have strong growth prospects, with Aerospace targeting 6%-8% annual sales growth through 2030 and the automation company targeting 4%-6% growth with over 10% annual EPS growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock price increased 4.4% on approval of strategic spinoff. Both resulting companies have strong growth drivers: aerospace benefits from commercial travel recovery and defense spending; automation benefits from energy security, decarbonization, and manufacturing reshoring initiatives. Management guidance shows solid growth targets with earnings growth exceeding sales growth.

Mentioned as a recent IPO spinoff from Honeywell's quantum computing business. No specific performance metrics or forward-looking statements provided in the article to warrant positive or negative sentiment.
